interfaces.name: RouteEndpoint
interfaces.signature: struct RouteEndpoint: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let label: String; let coordinate: Coordinate }
interfaces.notes: Used by Favorite, RecentDestination, and RouteSnapshot origin/destination.

interfaces.name: EndpointSource
interfaces.signature: enum EndpointSource: String, Codable, Sendable { case currentLocation, searchedPlace, droppedPin, favorite, recent, demo }
interfaces.notes: Used for analytics-free UI behavior and copy branching.

interfaces.name: ObstacleType
interfaces.signature: enum ObstacleType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case stairsNoRamp, steepGrade, unevenSurface, constructionBlockage, noCurbCut, temporaryHazard }
interfaces.notes: Fixed six-item vocabulary; scoring understands nothing outside this list.

interfaces.name: ObstacleSeverity
interfaces.signature: enum ObstacleSeverity: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case minor, moderate, severe }
interfaces.notes: Report sheet exposes as segmented/button group, never a slider.

interfaces.name: ObstacleProvenance
interfaces.signature: enum ObstacleProvenance: String, Codable, Sendable { case seeded, userEntered }
interfaces.notes: Separates demo/sample data from user-authored reports.

interfaces.name: ObstacleReport
interfaces.signature: struct ObstacleReport: Identifiable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var type: ObstacleType; var severity: ObstacleSeverity; var coordinate: Coordinate; var note: String?; var createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date; var provenance: ReportProvenance; var supersedes: UUID?; var isClearedReport: Bool; var isActiveForScoring: Bool }
interfaces.notes: isActiveForScoring false once superseded; row retained for history, excluded from scoring/matching.

interfaces.name: GeoCoordinate
interfaces.signature: struct GeoCoordinate: Equatable, Codable, Sendable { var latitude: Double; var longitude: Double }
interfaces.notes: Framework-neutral coordinate type for tests and persistence mapping.

interfaces.name: RoutePolylinePoint
interfaces.signature: struct RoutePolylinePoint: Equatable, Codable, Sendable { var latitude: Double; var longitude: Double }
interfaces.notes: Normalized route geometry point.

interfaces.name: RouteOption
interfaces.signature: struct RouteOption: Equatable, Codable, Sendable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; var expectedTravelTime: TimeInterval; var distanceMeters: Double; var polyline: [RoutePolylinePoint]; var advisoryNotices: [String] }
interfaces.notes: Single MapKit route translated into app-owned data.

interfaces.name: RouteSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct RouteSnapshot: Identifiable, Equatable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var origin: RouteEndpoint; var destination: RouteEndpoint; var fetchedAt: Date; var candidates: [RouteCandidate]; var source: RouteSnapshotSource }
interfaces.notes: Shared container for one fetched or cached route result; fetchedAt drives stale-cached-route honesty rules.

interfaces.name: RouteSnapshotSource
interfaces.signature: enum RouteSnapshotSource: String, Codable, Sendable { case live, cached, demo }
interfaces.notes: demo means the obstacle overlay is seeded, not that the route geometry is fake — the demo flow still does a real MapKit fetch.

interfaces.name: MatchedObstacle
interfaces.signature: struct MatchedObstacle: Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; var report: ObstacleReport; var minDistanceMeters: Double; var relationship: RouteRelationship; var routeProgress: Double }
interfaces.notes: Output of matching layer, sorted later into travel order.

interfaces.name: RouteRelationship
interfaces.signature: enum RouteRelationship: String, Sendable { case onRoute, nearby, ignored }
interfaces.notes: Implements 10m/20m threshold semantics.

interfaces.name: ConfidenceLevel
interfaces.signature: enum ConfidenceLevel: String, Codable, Sendable { case low, moderate, high }
interfaces.notes: Derived from report density/freshness, independent of match count; icon+word, never color-alone.

interfaces.name: FrictionLevel
interfaces.signature: enum FrictionLevel: String, Codable, Sendable { case low, moderate, high }
interfaces.notes: User-facing categorical score, not raw number.

interfaces.name: RouteExplanation
interfaces.signature: struct RouteExplanation: Equatable, Sendable { var recommendationSentence: String; var whySentence: String; var confidenceDetail: String; var warningChips: [WarningChipModel]; var routeStatusLine: String }
interfaces.notes: Structured deterministic copy, kept over a plain string since map/list/share/VoiceOver each need pieces separately.

interfaces.name: WarningChipModel
interfaces.signature: struct WarningChipModel: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var title: String; var kind: WarningChipKind; var count: Int }
interfaces.notes: Shared by map/list/share-preview surfaces.

interfaces.name: WarningChipKind
interfaces.signature: enum WarningChipKind: String, Sendable { case onRoute, nearby, staleData, demoData }
interfaces.notes: Supports trust messaging without overloading color.

interfaces.name: ScoredRoute
interfaces.signature: struct ScoredRoute: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var route: RouteCandidate; var matchedObstacles: [ObstacleMatch]; var frictionScore: Double; var frictionCategory: FrictionCategory; var confidence: ConfidenceLevel; var explanation: RouteExplanation; var isRecommended: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Fully converged shape, consumed by Route Result, Saved refresh, and share/export.

interfaces.name: FavoriteRoute
interfaces.signature: struct FavoriteRoute: Equatable, Codable, Sendable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; var label: String; var origin: RouteEndpoint; var destination: RouteEndpoint; var createdAt: Date; var lastOpenedAt: Date? }
interfaces.notes: Stored favorite without route geometry.

interfaces.name: RecentPlace
interfaces.signature: struct RecentPlace: Equatable, Codable, Sendable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; var endpoint: RouteEndpoint; var createdAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Persistence layer trims to five records.

interfaces.name: ShareSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct ShareSnapshot: Equatable, Codable, Sendable { let destinationLabel: String; let recommendationSentence: String; let confidence: ConfidenceLevel; let generatedAt: Date; let disclaimerText: String }
interfaces.notes: Self-contained payload for ShareLink; no account or network required to open.

interfaces.name: RoutingFailure
interfaces.signature: enum RoutingFailure: Error, Equatable, Sendable { case noConnectivity; case geocodingFailed(String); case noWalkingRoute; case directionsFailed(String); case cancelled }
interfaces.notes: Keeps no-network, geocoding, and no-route states distinct.

interfaces.name: RouteFetching
interfaces.signature: protocol RouteFetching: Sendable { func fetchRoutes(from origin: RouteEndpoint, to destination: RouteEndpoint) async throws -> RouteSnapshot }
interfaces.notes: Live impl wraps MKDirections; throws RouteRequestError, never raw MKError. Tests inject a fake, never touch real MapKit.

interfaces.name: PlaceSearching
interfaces.signature: protocol PlaceSearching: Sendable { func search(query: String, near origin: Coordinate?) async throws -> [RouteEndpoint] }
interfaces.notes: Kept separate from RouteFetching so geocoding/search failures stay a distinct code path from routing failures.

interfaces.name: LocationProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol LocationProviding: AnyObject, Sendable { var authorizationState: LocationAuthorizationState { get }; func requestWhenInUseAuthorization(); func currentLocation() async throws -> Coordinate }
interfaces.notes: Observed state, not polled; supports permission-revoked-mid-session.

interfaces.name: LocationAuthorizationState
interfaces.signature: enum LocationAuthorizationState: Equatable, Sendable { case notDetermined, denied, restricted, authorizedApproximate, authorizedPrecise }
interfaces.notes: authorizedApproximate can put a pedestrian's origin 100m+ off, on the wrong side of a block — LocationService/Plan should nudge toward manual pin-drop rather than treat it as equivalent to precise. Not blocking for MVP but named so it isn't silently ignored.

interfaces.name: ObstacleStore
interfaces.signature: protocol ObstacleStore { func installSeedDataIfNeeded() async throws; func resetSeedData() async throws; func fetchActiveReports() async throws -> [ObstacleReport]; func saveReport(_ report: ObstacleReport) async throws; func supersede(existingID: UUID, with newReport: ObstacleReport) async throws; func supersedeNearby(coordinate: Coordinate, thresholdMeters: Double, with newReport: ObstacleReport) async throws; func fetchHistory(near coordinate: Coordinate, thresholdMeters: Double) async throws -> [ObstacleReport] }
interfaces.notes: installSeedDataIfNeeded checks ScoringConstants.currentSeedDataVersion against a persisted installed-version flag so an app update can revise demo content without duplicating rows. resetSeedData backs the locked Settings 'demo-data reset' control.

interfaces.name: FavoritesStore
interfaces.signature: protocol FavoritesStore { func all() async throws -> [Favorite]; func add(_ favorite: Favorite) async throws; func remove(id: UUID) async throws; func count() async throws -> Int }
interfaces.notes: count() checked before save so the 3rd attempt triggers paywall cleanly, never a half-save.

interfaces.name: RecentsStore
interfaces.signature: protocol RecentsStore { func fetchRecents(limit: Int) async throws -> [RecentDestination]; func recordRecent(_ endpoint: RouteEndpoint) async throws; func clearRecents() async throws }
interfaces.notes: Backs the explicit 'clear recents' Settings control, no hidden movement trail.

interfaces.name: CachedRouteStore
interfaces.signature: protocol CachedRouteStore { func loadCachedRoute(origin: RouteEndpoint, destination: RouteEndpoint) async throws -> RouteSnapshot?; func saveCachedRoute(_ snapshot: RouteSnapshot) async throws; func deleteExpiredRoutes(olderThan: Date) async throws }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED BY COORDINATOR: Codex's round-3 argument to keep deleteExpiredRoutes wins — cached-route honesty is a locked product requirement and expiry is part of that lifecycle, not just storage housekeeping; naming it means one lane owns it.

interfaces.name: PurchaseManaging
interfaces.signature: protocol PurchaseManaging: AnyObject { var hasProUnlock: Bool { get }; func purchasePro() async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws }
interfaces.notes: Non-consumable product id com.clearpathaccess.pro.lifetime, on-device VerificationResult only, no receipt-validation backend.

interfaces.name: ReachabilityProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ReachabilityProviding { var isNetworkReachable: Bool { get } }
interfaces.notes: NWPathMonitor-backed coarse hint, never sole error-cause truth source.

interfaces.name: ObstacleMatching
interfaces.signature: protocol ObstacleMatching { func matchObstacles(to route: RouteOption, reports: [ObstacleReport]) -> [MatchedObstacle] }
interfaces.notes: Pure domain logic for 10m/20m thresholds and route progress ordering.

interfaces.name: RouteScoring
interfaces.signature: protocol RouteScoring { func score(snapshot: RouteSnapshot, reports: [ObstacleReport], now: Date) -> [ScoredRoute] }
interfaces.notes: Pure domain logic for friction, confidence, recommendation, and tie-break.

interfaces.name: RouteExplanationBuilding
interfaces.signature: protocol RouteExplanationBuilding { func buildExplanation(for scoredRoutes: [ScoredRoute], selectedRouteID: UUID?, snapshot: RouteSnapshot, now: Date) -> RouteExplanation }
interfaces.notes: Separates copy generation from friction math so wording can evolve without destabilizing tests around scoring.

interfaces.name: makeShareSnapshot
interfaces.signature: func makeShareSnapshot(from route: ScoredRoute, destination: RouteEndpoint, generatedAt: Date, disclaimerText: String) -> ShareSnapshot
interfaces.notes: Stable builder used by share preview and ShareLink export.

interfaces.name: rescoreCurrentSnapshot
interfaces.signature: func rescoreCurrentSnapshot(_ snapshot: RouteSnapshot, reports: [ObstacleReport], scorer: RouteScoring, now: Date) -> [ScoredRoute]
interfaces.notes: Critical local-only update path after report submission.

interfaces.name: Coordinate
interfaces.signature: struct Coordinate: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let latitude: Double; let longitude: Double }
interfaces.notes: Single shared stand-in for CLLocationCoordinate2D across all lanes.

interfaces.name: ReportProvenance
interfaces.signature: enum ReportProvenance: String, Codable, Sendable { case seeded, userEntered }
interfaces.notes: Drives DemoBadge/dashed-outline treatment everywhere seeded data renders.

interfaces.name: RoutePolyline
interfaces.signature: struct RoutePolyline: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let points: [Coordinate] }
interfaces.notes: Owned copy of route geometry; never re-derived from a live MKRoute after initial fetch.

interfaces.name: RouteCandidate
interfaces.signature: struct RouteCandidate: Identifiable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var polyline: RoutePolyline; var distanceMeters: Double; var expectedTravelTimeSeconds: Double; var stepSummaries: [String]; var advisoryNotices: [String] }
interfaces.notes: Converted immediately from MKRoute at the RouteFetching boundary. advisoryNotices carries MKRoute.advisoryNotices verbatim (empty array when MapKit returns none); optional, low-stakes, no UI consumer required in MVP.

interfaces.name: ObstacleRelationship
interfaces.signature: enum ObstacleRelationship: String, Codable, Sendable { case onRoute, nearby }
interfaces.notes: onRoute <=10m, nearby 10-20m. Two cases only: beyond 20m never becomes an ObstacleMatch, so a third 'ignored' case would model an unreachable state.

interfaces.name: ObstacleMatch
interfaces.signature: struct ObstacleMatch: Identifiable, Hashable, Sendable { var id: UUID { report.id }; let report: ObstacleReport; let distanceMeters: Double; let relationship: ObstacleRelationship; let routeProgress: Double }
interfaces.notes: routeProgress (0...1) sorts the accessible list view in travel order; comes from the matcher, never recomputed in UI.

interfaces.name: FrictionCategory
interfaces.signature: enum FrictionCategory: String, Codable, Sendable { case low, moderate, high }
interfaces.notes: Categorical label shown to user; raw numeric score never surfaced directly.

interfaces.name: RouteScore
interfaces.signature: struct RouteScore: Hashable { let rawFrictionValue: Double; let frictionCategory: FrictionCategory; let confidence: ConfidenceLevel; let matchedObstacles: [ObstacleMatch]; let explanation: String }
interfaces.notes: explanation is template-generated plain language from the matched obstacles/confidence inputs, not LLM-generated.

interfaces.name: RouteRecommendation
interfaces.signature: struct RouteRecommendation: Equatable, Sendable { let scoredCandidates: [ScoredRoute]; let recommendedIndex: Int; let isSingleRouteResult: Bool }
interfaces.notes: isSingleRouteResult drives honest single-route wording and hides route-switcher chips.

interfaces.name: ScoringConstants
interfaces.signature: enum ScoringConstants { static let outerBufferMeters: Double = 20; static let innerBufferMeters: Double = 10; static func recencyMultiplier(daysOld: Int) -> Double; static let typeBaseWeight: [ObstacleType: Double]; static let severityMultiplier: [ObstacleSeverity: Double]; static let highConfidenceMinReports: Int = 3; static let highConfidenceRecentWithinDays: Int = 30; static let currentSeedDataVersion: Int = 1 }
interfaces.notes: daysOld is Int (whole days) so day 6/8, 30/31, 90/91 boundary tests can't drift on floating-point seconds. Bands: <7d=1.0, 8-30d=0.6, 31-90d=0.3, >90d=0.1. Types: stairsNoRamp 3.0, noCurbCut 2.5, constructionBlockage 2.5, steepGrade 2.0, unevenSurface 1.5, temporaryHazard 1.0. Severity: minor 1.0, moderate 1.75, severe 2.5.

interfaces.name: RouteScorer
interfaces.signature: enum RouteScorer { static func score(candidate: RouteCandidate, allReports: [ObstacleReport], now: Date) -> ScoredRoute; static func recommend(candidates: [RouteCandidate], allReports: [ObstacleReport], now: Date) -> RouteRecommendation }
interfaces.notes: Static pure function, not a protocol: one correct algorithm, no legitimate fake. Applies shorter-route tie-break when friction categories tie.

interfaces.name: ObstacleMatcher
interfaces.signature: enum ObstacleMatcher { static func match(reports: [ObstacleReport], to polyline: RoutePolyline) -> [ObstacleMatch] }
interfaces.notes: Bounding-box pre-filter by outerBufferMeters before per-segment projection math so cost doesn't grow against the whole obstacle store.

interfaces.name: RouteRequestError
interfaces.signature: enum RouteRequestError: Error, Equatable, Sendable { case noConnectivity; case geocodingFailed(String); case noRouteFound; case directionsFailed(String); case cancelled }
interfaces.notes: cancelled needed for search-debounce .task(id:) cancellation. Never collapse into one generic error banner.

interfaces.name: ObstacleRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol ObstacleRepository { func allReports() throws -> [ObstacleReport]; func save(_ report: ObstacleReport) throws; func supersede(existingID: UUID, with newReport: ObstacleReport) throws }
interfaces.notes: SwiftDataObstacleRepository is the live impl; mapping to/from ObstacleReportRecord happens only inside this implementation.

interfaces.name: FavoritesRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol FavoritesRepository { func all() throws -> [Favorite]; func add(_ favorite: Favorite) throws; func remove(id: UUID) throws; var count: Int { get throws } }
interfaces.notes: count is checked before save to trigger the paywall on the 3rd attempt (free cap = 2).

interfaces.name: Favorite
interfaces.signature: struct Favorite: Identifiable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var label: String; var origin: RouteEndpoint; var destination: RouteEndpoint; var createdAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED: lastOpenedAt dropped — nothing in the locked spec sorts or displays favorites by last-opened time, so the field had no consumer anywhere in the contract.

interfaces.name: CachedRouteSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct CachedRouteSnapshot: Hashable { let favoriteID: UUID; let candidate: RouteCandidate; let fetchedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: staleness threshold ~1 day; past it, UI copy shifts from 'today's suggested route' to 'your last available route, from [day]'.

interfaces.name: RecentDestination
interfaces.signature: struct RecentDestination: Identifiable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var endpoint: RouteEndpoint; var createdAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Capped at 5, label+coordinate only, no per-search timestamp trail beyond the entry's own createdAt.

interfaces.name: PurchaseServicing
interfaces.signature: protocol PurchaseServicing: AnyObject { var isProUnlocked: Bool { get }; func purchasePro() async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws }
interfaces.notes: StoreKit 2, non-consumable product id com.clearpathaccess.pro.lifetime, on-device VerificationResult only, no receipt-validation backend.

interfaces.name: ReachabilityMonitoring
interfaces.signature: protocol ReachabilityMonitoring { var isConnected: Bool { get } }
interfaces.notes: NWPathMonitor-backed; used only to help classify RouteRequestError.noConnectivity, not as sole error-cause truth source.

interfaces.name: RouteResultInput
interfaces.signature: struct RouteResultInput: Hashable, Sendable { let origin: RouteEndpoint; let destination: RouteEndpoint; let favoriteID: UUID?; let isDemoCorridor: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Single navigation payload shared by Plan, Saved, and the demo card. No alternate push models.

interfaces.name: PlanRoute
interfaces.signature: enum PlanRoute: Hashable { case search; case result(RouteResultInput) }
interfaces.notes: NavigationStack path type for the Plan tab.

interfaces.name: SavedRoute
interfaces.signature: enum SavedRoute: Hashable { case result(RouteResultInput) }
interfaces.notes: Independent NavigationStack path for Saved, separate from Plan.

interfaces.name: RouteResultLoadPhase
interfaces.signature: enum RouteResultLoadPhase: Equatable { case fetchingRoute; case checkingObstacles; case loaded; case failed(RouteRequestError) }
interfaces.notes: checkingObstacles holds a visible 400-600ms minimum even when local scoring is instant.

interfaces.name: RouteResultViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class RouteResultViewModel { var phase: RouteResultLoadPhase; var recommendation: RouteRecommendation?; var currentSnapshot: RouteSnapshot?; var segment: Segment; func load(_ input: RouteResultInput) async; func rescore() }
interfaces.notes: rescore() is the only post-report mutation path, never calls RouteFetching again; report sheet calls ReportSubmission.submit then rescore(). Must recover cleanly from scene-phase backgrounding/process death by reloading, never showing stale/garbage state.

interfaces.name: FavoriteRowStatus
interfaces.signature: enum FavoriteRowStatus: Equatable { case idle; case checking; case upToDate; case newIssues(count: Int); case offlineUnavailable(lastKnown: Date?) }
interfaces.notes: Per-row Saved state; concurrent checks capped at 2, triggered from row .onAppear, never all at once.

interfaces.name: hasCompletedFirstRouteSearch
interfaces.signature: var hasCompletedFirstRouteSearch: Bool { get set } // UserDefaults-backed flag
interfaces.notes: Persisted one-time flag gating the Plan demo card; never derived from zero-recents/zero-favorites state.

interfaces.name: RouteExplanationBuilder
interfaces.signature: enum RouteExplanationBuilder { static func build(for scoredRoutes: [ScoredRoute], selectedRouteID: UUID?, snapshot: RouteSnapshot, now: Date) -> RouteExplanation }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED as a static pure function, not a protocol: both agents independently converged — deterministic copy generation from known inputs has no legitimate reason to be faked, same rule as RouteScorer/ObstacleMatcher.

interfaces.name: ReportDraft
interfaces.signature: struct ReportDraft: Equatable, Sendable { var type: ObstacleType; var severity: ObstacleSeverity; var note: String?; var coordinate: Coordinate; var looksClearNow: Bool; var targetObstacleID: UUID? }
interfaces.notes: targetObstacleID set only when reporting against an already-flagged item (adds the 'looks clear now' path).

interfaces.name: ReportSubmission
interfaces.signature: enum ReportSubmission { static func submit(_ draft: ReportDraft, store: ObstacleStore) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Owns the decision of which ObstacleStore method to call: supersede(existingID:) if targetObstacleID is set; else supersedeNearby using ScoringConstants.innerBufferMeters (10m — the same-physical-obstacle check, deliberately not the 20m outer route-relevance buffer, since those are different questions and using 20m risks superseding an unrelated obstacle two doors down); else saveReport. Report sheet's completion handler should only ever call this plus routeResultViewModel.rescore().

interfaces.name: AppDependencies
interfaces.signature: struct AppDependencies { let routeFetching: RouteFetching; let placeSearching: PlaceSearching; let locationProviding: LocationProviding; let obstacleStore: ObstacleStore; let favoritesStore: FavoritesStore; let recentsStore: RecentsStore; let cachedRouteStore: CachedRouteStore; let purchaseManaging: PurchaseManaging; let reachabilityProviding: ReachabilityProviding }
interfaces.notes: Single composition-root bundle built once in App/ and injected via environment so no build lane wires up ad hoc service instances.

interfaces.name: Segment
interfaces.signature: enum Segment: String, CaseIterable, Sendable { case map, list }
interfaces.notes: Route Result's Map/List segmented control state; fills a real gap where RouteResultViewModel referenced this type without it ever being defined.
